current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is tough under very warm water conditions at 76.6°F. Recent stocking provides some confidence using attractor nymphs and egg patterns.
generated_text.best_window: Day 2 morning is best because lower temperatures near 71°F and high humidity improve trout comfort, supporting a balanced nymph approach particularly with stocked-trout patterns.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 76.6°F, flow is 105 cfs, gage height is 3.75 ft, and turbidity is 11.3 NTU at the Etowah River USGS site.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 23% with pressure steady at 30.0 inHg; winds are light at 4 mph from the west with no expected rain.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor cold-water refuges, oxygenated runs, seams, and structure, often staying close to cover to manage stress from warm water. Present smaller natural patterns with clean drifts and include stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns and squirmy worms, but consider not targeting trout aggressively due to conditions.
